seoTitle: How to Change Microsoft Loop Language Settings
metaDescription: Switch the display and spell-check language in Microsoft Loop using a URL locale parameter or your Microsoft account's preferred language settings.
intro: A mismatched interface language or spell-checker can slow down technical writing and trigger distracting typo highlights. This guide covers two ways to change your Microsoft Loop language — a quick URL-based override and a more permanent account-level setting.

steps.heading: Try the URL locale override
steps.body: Navigate to loop.cloud.microsoft and add a locale parameter, such as ?locale=en-us, to instantly force a specific language layout.

steps.heading: Refresh the desktop app if needed
steps.body: If you're using the installed Progressive Web App version, press Ctrl+R or Cmd+R to force it to match the new URL-based language setting.

steps.heading: Update your account language preferences
steps.body: Navigate to your primary Microsoft account settings page and open the Settings & Privacy section.

steps.heading: Reorder your preferred languages
steps.body: Move your target writing language to the top of the Preferred Languages list to lock it in as the application default.

steps.heading: Sign out and back in
steps.body: Return to your main workspace and sign out, then sign back in to let the changes take effect across your menus and spell-checker.

faq.question: Will changing my Loop language also affect spell check?
faq.answer: Yes, your account's preferred language settings influence both the interface text and the spell-check dictionary used in Loop.

faq.question: Do I need to restart the app after changing the language?
faq.answer: Signing out and back in, or refreshing the app, is usually needed for the new language to fully take effect.

faq.question: Can I change the language just for one session without affecting my account?
faq.answer: Yes, using a URL locale parameter gives you a temporary override without changing your permanent account settings.

faq.question: Will date and currency formats change along with the language?
faq.answer: Regional formatting like dates and currency is tied to your account's language and region settings, so changing your preferred language can affect those too.
